title: Set up direct deposit for NIS payments
description: Have your National Insurance benefit paid directly into your bank or credit union account.
summary: Most NIS benefits and pensions can be paid by direct deposit into your bank or credit union account. This is faster, safer and means you do not have to collect a paper cheque.

To set it up, you need to give the National Insurance Office the details of your account. The simplest way is to bring a copy of the header from your account – the document that shows your name, your account number and the branch.
Most banks and credit unions can print this header for you on request. Some online banking apps let you download it as a PDF.
Once your form is processed, the next payment will normally go straight to your account. If you change banks or accounts later, submit a new Direct Deposit Form to update the record.

items: your National Insurance Number and claim number (if you have one)
your full name, address and phone number
the name of your bank or credit union

items.name: Bank or credit union account header
items.note: Showing your name and account number. You can usually get this from your branch or from online banking.

how_long: Direct deposit is usually active within 1 to 2 payment cycles.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Direct Deposit Form to the National Insurance Office.

assumptions: ‘Direct deposit is usually active within 1 to 2 payment cycles’ is an estimate.
Description of how to get an account header (banking app, branch) is general guidance, not specific to any one Barbadian bank.
